nl_query: For each audit log entry related to a result correction, show the result ID, the old marks, the new marks, the name of the user who made the correction, the reason given, and the time elapsed since the previous correction on the same result.
failed_sql: SELECT r.id AS result_id, em_old.marks_awarded AS old_evaluation_marks, em_new.marks_awarded AS new_evaluation_marks, au.display_name AS actor, al.reason AS reason, (al.created_at - al.previous_correction.created_at) AS time_elapsed FROM result r JOIN evaluation_marks em_old ON em_old.attempt_id IN (SELECT ea.id FROM evaluation_attempt ea WHERE ea.result_id = r.id AND ea.is_final = TRUE AND ea.status = 'FROZEN' AND ea.evaluation_type = 'PRIMARY') JOIN evaluation_marks em_new ON em_new.attempt_id IN (SELECT ea.id FROM evaluation_attempt ea WHERE ea.result_id = r.id AND ea.is_final = TRUE AND ea.status = 'FROZEN' AND ea.evaluation_type = 'PRIMARY') JOIN app_user au ON au.id = em_new.evaluator_user_id JOIN audit_log al ON al.entity_type = 'EVALUATION_ATTEMPT' AND al.entity_id = em_new.attempt_id LEFT JOIN audit_log al_previous_correction ON al_previous_correction.entity_type = 'EVALUATION_ATTEMPT' AND al_previous_correction.entity_id = em_new.attempt_id AND al_previous_correction.reason = 'Correction made' AND al_previous_correction.created_at < al.created_at ORDER BY al.created_at DESC LIMIT 100;
error: Validation failed (schema): Hallucinated column(s): evaluation_marks.evaluator_user_id, evaluation_attempt.evaluation_type, evaluation_attempt.evaluation_type, evaluation_attempt.result_id, evaluation_attempt.is_final. Use only columns that exist in the schema.
